sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.text: Sarah Johnson (Senior Product Manager) has completed an enterprise competency assessment across 5 capability domains. The overall competency score is 77/100 (level 4). Employability Index stands at 81.2/100 (Excellent). Top strength: Communication Readiness (84). Most material development gap: Agile Collaboration. Match to Senior Product Manager is 77.4%.

sections.text: This summary consolidates the candidate’s strongest evidence across competency, employability and role-fit lenses. Each headline metric is explored in its dedicated report; figures are developmental signals, not hiring or promotion decisions.

sections.title: Confidence Level
sections.text: Overall confidence: Provisional. Domain-proxy scoring (aggregate domain level, not per-competency). Confidence rises to High once a competency-tagged assessment is scored. Coverage, Confidence and Activation are reported as separate axes and are never combined into a single figure.

sections.title: Assessment Summary
sections.text: Mean domain-proxy score by competency type — Behavioral: 80.4; Cognitive: 82; Functional: 74; Technical: 74.

sections.title: Interpretation
sections.text: The radar contrasts capability across competency types (behavioural, cognitive, functional, technical, future-skills). Each type mean is a domain-proxy: every competency inherits the measured score of its onto-domain. A balanced shape indicates broad capability; a skewed shape highlights concentration.

insights.text: Strongest: Cognitive (82).
Development priority: Technical (74).

sections.title: Confidence Level
sections.text: Derived from 100% type-classification coverage (structural; not measured scores).

sections.title: Data Source
sections.text: competency-runtime.computeTypeProfile → onto_competency_profiles domain scores, grouped by competency type (domain-PROXY mean per type; an onto-domain with no bank code, e.g. strategic, is unmeasurable and excluded).

sections.title: Assessment Summary
sections.text: Recommendations span 6 type(s). Top item: Target role: AI Product Manager.

sections.title: Visualizations
sections.text: No chartable measured data points are available for this report yet; the metrics above summarise everything currently measured.

sections.title: Interpretation
sections.text: Recommendations are derived developmental suggestions ranked by relevance. Personalized items consume the candidate’s own profile; catalog items are general and flagged as provisional. None are directives.

insights.text: Target role: AI Product Manager — A strong current market signal (demand and growth) for AI Product Manager in the product function.
Advance from a Advanced readiness base — Your present-state readiness is at the Advanced band. Consolidating Cognitive Development is the highest-leverage next move on your current track.
Consider the research industry — The research industry shows strong aggregate role demand and growth in the catalog. (general)
Consider the data function — The data function shows strong aggregate demand and growth across catalog roles. (general)
Future-resilient role: GenAI Engineer — GenAI Engineer combines high projected growth with lower automation exposure — a more future-resilient target. (general)
Alternative direction: GenAI Engineer — GenAI Engineer sits in the data function — a different direction from your current track, with healthy market signal. (general)

sections.title: Confidence Level
sections.text: Confidence varies per recommendation; personalized items inherit the chain confidence, catalog items are Provisional.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.text: Sarah Johnson's action plan organises development into 5 streams.

sections.title: Candidate Information
sections.text:
Name: Sarah Johnson
Reference (masked): user_4286d980cc6c
Current role: Senior Product Manager
Target role: Director of Product
Industry: Technology
Experience: 8 years
Education: MBA
Location: Bangalore, India

Profile summary: Senior Product Manager with 8 years across B2B SaaS, leading cross-functional teams and data-driven product strategy. MBA. Seeking director-level product leadership.

sections.title: Assessment Summary
sections.text: Streams — Behavioral Development (0 comps); Technical Development (0 comps); Functional Development (0 comps); Cognitive Development (1 comps, ~4w); Future Skills Development (0 comps).

sections.title: Interpretation
sections.text: The action plan groups development into themed streams, each addressing a cluster of related gaps with a now/next/later horizon. Tracking compares effort over time once a baseline exists.

insights.text: Behavioral Development: How you work with others and regulate yourself — interpersonal, attitudinal and self-management behaviours..
Technical Development: Tool, technology and domain-specific technical proficiency..
Functional Development: Role and process execution capabilities that deliver work outcomes..

sections.title: Confidence Level
sections.text: Confidence: Provisional — depends on gap and requirement coverage.
